caption: Golden hour never gets old ✨ Shot this on my walk home through the Mission. The light was absolutely unreal today.
Third cup of coffee and I'm finally feeling human again. My espresso setup took 2 years to dial in. Worth every hour.

Finally released v1.0 of my open source CLI tool after 6 months of evenings and weekends. The bug in line 847 of the parser haunted my dreams. It's done. It ships. 🎉

Repo link in bio.
Late night jam session. Working on a riff I've had stuck in my head for three weeks. It finally clicked tonight at 2am.

New illustration series: 'Liminal Spaces'. This is piece 1 of 12. Each one explores the feeling of being between two places — physically and emotionally.

DM for print info.
Process video: from sketch to final illustration. I use a 6-layer system that took years to develop. Sharing the full breakdown in my newsletter this week.
22 miles on Mt. Tam this morning. Legs are cooked. Heart is full. The fog was sitting perfectly in the valley — photos don't do it justice but I tried.

New PR at the Oakland Half Marathon: 1:21:43! 3 minutes off my previous best. The training block from Lydiard base training finally clicked.

Full race report on my Substack.

Brown butter miso pasta with crispy capers. The combination of umami from the miso and nuttiness from the brown butter is borderline criminal.

Full recipe in the comments ↓
Market haul from the Ferry Building today. Spring produce is finally here — I've been dreaming about English peas and fava beans for months.
Monstera deliciosa update: she put out three new leaves this month after I switched to rainwater. Plants know.
60 minutes of yin yoga this morning and I feel like I've been wrung out like a wet towel — in the best way. Tension I didn't know I was holding.

One year of building this project in public. Thread on what I learned ↓

1/ Ship before you feel ready. I waited 4 months too long on v0.1.
My desk setup after the big reorganization. Finally got the cable chaos under control. The monitor arm made a ridiculous difference in how spacious the desk feels.
Shot this for a brand campaign last week. The brief was 'controlled chaos' and I think we nailed it. Grateful for a team that trusts the process.
Portrait study series no. 7. Available light only. No reflectors. Just a window and patience.
Finished 'The Remains of the Day' at 2am last night. I've been staring at the ceiling since. Stevens is the most devastating portrait of self-deception in fiction and I can't shake it.
My 'currently reading' pile for the month. Yes, I read them simultaneously. No, I don't feel bad about it.
Fog rolling into the bay from Twin Peaks. This is why I moved to San Francisco ten years ago and still haven't left.
Piece 2 of 12 in the Liminal Spaces series: 'Departure Lounge at 4am'. There's something about airports at that hour that feels like a pause between lives.
